stories.body: Iran has begun a six-day funeral procession for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, drawing vast crowds to Tehran’s Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla where mourners chanted “Death to America” and “Revenge” as his flag-draped coffin, flanked by those of relatives killed in the February U.S.-Israeli strike, lay on a Kaaba-like platform before tours to Qom, Najaf, Kerbala and a final burial in Mashhad, with authorities promising free transport, lodging and food to mobilize millions even as his wounded son and successor Mojtaba remains out of view.
stories.headline: Iran stages sprawling week-long farewell to slain Supreme Leader while crowds vow revenge

stories.body: Russia’s military told President Vladimir Putin it has seized Kostiantynivka, a key transport and industrial hub in Donetsk, and is pressing toward Lyman while seeking larger buffer zones inside Ukraine to counter intensified long-range strikes, even as commanders admit they have not fully stemmed Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian oil sites and Ukraine stays silent on the loss; meanwhile Putin and Volodymyr Zelensky each spoke with U.S. President Donald Trump ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara as diplomacy remains stalled.
stories.headline: Russia claims capture of Kostiantynivka, signals wider security zones as Kyiv hits back

stories.body: After shooting down Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. forces struck surveillance radar sites at Goruk and Qeshm Island, underscoring how intermittent ceasefires have failed to stop flare-ups even as Washington and Tehran negotiate indirectly over reopening the chokepoint and unlocking Iranian oil revenues; Iran, which has largely shut Hormuz since late February, is reviewing a proposed interim deal while demanding Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon and warning it could extend blockades to Bab el-Mandeb if attacks resume, all against a backdrop of rising fuel prices, tight global inventories and stepped-up Hezbollah-Israel clashes.
stories.headline: U.S.-Iran war drags on as Hormuz truce talks stall and tit-for-tat strikes continue

stories.body: Tehran publicly reaffirmed support for Hezbollah, insisting Israel must withdraw from southern Lebanon before it will accept a broader ceasefire to end the U.S.-Iran war and reopen Hormuz, even as Iranian officials warned Israel against renewed strikes on Beirut and the navy said it fired warning shots at U.S. destroyers, while in Istanbul Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan said Israel must not be allowed to “dynamite” the fragile U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding and urged regional ownership of any peace plan.
stories.headline: Iran ties any regional ceasefire to Israeli pullout from Lebanon while Erdogan warns against derailing U.S.-Iran pact

stories.body: President Donald Trump plans to headline Washington’s fenced-off National Mall with what he calls the “most spectacular TRUMP RALLY,” featuring military flyovers and massive fireworks as part of the country’s 250th anniversary, even as extreme heat temporarily shut the Great American State Fair, crowds flag the event’s politicized tone, and groups such as the white nationalist Patriot Front arrive alongside protesters while other cities mark the holiday with more traditional festivities.
stories.headline: Trump centers U.S. 250th celebrations on partisan rally despite heat, storms and security jitters

stories.body: In a 52-47 vote with no Democratic support, Senate Republicans approved $70 billion in extra funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol to underwrite President Trump’s three-year deportation drive, while rejecting efforts to permanently kill a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that Democrats call a slush fund for Trump allies; moderates such as Susan Collins briefly sided with Democrats, other Republicans sought alternative amendments to redirect or codify limits, and House action could come next week amid intraparty unease ahead of the midterms.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill without axing Trump’s compensation fund

stories.body: Dozens of Patriot Front members in masks, sunglasses and tan caps paraded through Washington’s Eastern Market and Capitol Hill carrying Confederate, Patriot Front and inverted U.S. flags while chanting “Life, liberty, victory,” prompting D.C. police to monitor the demonstration as tourists headed to Trump’s “Salute to America,” and highlighting the administration’s heavy focus on spectacle amid counterprotests, heightened security and lingering criticism of the 250th festivities’ political overtones.
stories.headline: White supremacist Patriot Front march raises July 4 security concerns in Washington

stories.body: A new Politico Poll finds roughly 60% of Americans believe politics now intrudes where it doesn’t belong, yet majorities still view celebrities, athletes and even local businesses more favorably when their statements align with personal views, revealing a paradox where voters decry politicization of sports and entertainment but reward aligned messages—especially among self-described MAGA Republicans and strong Democrats—while many celebrities grow reluctant to wade into campaigns after recent backlash.
stories.headline: Poll shows Americans weary of politics invading daily life even as partisan cues still sway them

stories.body: Andalusian premier Juan Manuel Moreno has granted Vox a vice presidency and sweeping policy concessions through a 150-point deal that pivots from his centrist branding to embrace hard-line planks such as “national priority” on social benefits, mass tax cuts totaling €1.3 billion over four years, elimination of environmental levies, stricter immigration controls, and curbs on subsidies to NGOs, prompting unions, environmentalists and Andalusian nationalists to warn the pact undermines regional identity and minority protections.
stories.headline: PP brings Vox into Andalusian government under 150-point pact cementing far-right agenda

stories.body: Judge Santiago Pedraz has charged Guardia Civil director Mercedes González and Deputy Operational Director Rafael López Malo with malfeasance and obstruction amid accusations that they coordinated with former Socialist operative Leire Díez to pressure the elite UCO unit investigating cases involving the PSOE, the government and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s family, a scandal that saw agents raid headquarters for visitor logs, uncover internal probes into UCO officers and force González to admit private meetings even as the government insists it still trusts both officials.
stories.headline: Guardia Civil director and operational chief charged over alleged meddling in ‘Leire’ corruption probe

stories.body: During closing arguments in the Kitchen case, Spain’s anti-corruption prosecutors described a 2013 “parapolice” operation allegedly orchestrated by Mariano Rajoy’s Interior Ministry to spy on former PP treasurer Luis Bárcenas and seize documents that could implicate senior party figures in the Gürtel scandal, citing text messages, audio recordings and seized agendas as evidence the scheme aimed to obstruct justice and protect top officials.
stories.headline: Prosecutors outline alleged Rajoy-era spy plot in Kitchen trial

stories.body: People’s Party leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo’s attempt to “turn the page” on the Catalan procés and open dialogue with Junts has angered party hawks, with former prime minister José María Aznar and Madrid leader Isabel Díaz Ayuso signaling clear unease over the 180-degree shift as internal critics warn that softening the PP’s stance on Catalan independence risks eroding its identity just as it partners with Vox in multiple regions.
stories.headline: Feijóo’s outreach to Junts meets resistance from PP hardliners

stories.body: Catalan firefighters say the Gavarres blaze has been stabilized after scorching roughly 2,300 hectares and destroying or damaging about 16 homes plus several industrial buildings around Calonge and Santa Cristina d’Aro, yet they remain focused on the irregular right flank as a heatwave and sea breezes threaten reignitions, prompting authorities to keep interior roadblocks, discourage “fire tourism,” and conduct structural damage assessments while preparing to lift some confinements.
stories.headline: Gavarres wildfire stabilised at 2,300 hectares but firefighters brace for heat and shifting winds

stories.body: With more than 130 municipalities at very high fire danger and the Gavarres area under level 4 Plan Alfa restrictions, Catalonia has kept the INFOCAT emergency active, shut access to sensitive forests, suspended all authorized burns and banned spark-producing machinery, fireworks and rural travel in key zones as a second heatwave approaches, urging residents to follow Agents Rurals guidance on what activities are prohibited at each risk level.
stories.headline: Catalonia activates maximum Plan Alfa restrictions amid extreme wildfire risk

stories.body: Barcelona’s government has approved the reparcelation plan for the Sector Prim area of Verneda i la Pau, clearing the way to demolish remaining industrial warehouses by 2027 and build 3,359 homes—2,041 on municipal land, including 1,637 protected units and 200 supportive dwellings—alongside 21,600 square meters of civic facilities such as a health center, nursery, expanded high school and sports hall that will anchor a new neighborhood linked to the future La Sagrera multimodal station and Parc del Camí Comtal.
stories.headline: Barcelona finalizes Sector Prim redevelopment, unlocking more than 2,000 public homes near La Sagrera

stories.body: The Fundació Joan Miró’s board unanimously chose cultural manager and art historian Maribel López Zambrana—best known for directing ARCOmadrid since 2019—from 29 candidates to become the museum’s next director in October 2026, succeeding Marko Daniel and tasked with steering the institution’s artistic and executive strategy while deepening ties with Barcelona’s cultural ecosystem.
stories.headline: Maribel López Zambrana to lead Fundació Joan Miró starting in October
